title: 970 Russian Losses in 24 Hours; Advances Near Hulyaipole as Ukraine Pushes Back Near Slovyansk
detail: Ukraine's General Staff reported 970 Russian personnel losses in the 24-hour period ending March 31, bringing cumulative total to approximately 1,297,670 since February 24, 2022 — Day 1,496 of the full-scale invasion. 147 combat clashes were recorded across the front. Ukrainian forces made tactical advances near Slovyansk and in the Kostyantynivka-Druzhkivka area. Russian forces advanced near Hulyaipole in Zaporizhzhia Oblast and in the Kostyantynivka-Druzhkivka tactical area, continuing pressure along the Fortress Belt agglomeration. Intense fighting continued in Sumy, Kharkiv, Slovyansk, Kostiantynivka, and Zaporizhzhia sectors. Russia deployed 9,355 kamikaze drones (including frontline FPVs) over 24 hours, conducted 70 airstrikes, and dropped 237 guided aerial bombs. One Russian air defense system was destroyed.

title: Zelensky Proposes Easter Ceasefire and Energy Infrastructure Halt; Secures Gulf State Security Agreements
detail: President Zelensky called for an Easter ceasefire with Russia ahead of Orthodox Easter on April 16, 2026, proposing a mutual halt on strikes against energy infrastructure. Zelensky stated Ukraine is 'ready for Easter ceasefire and energy ceasefire' and 'open to ceasefires in any form — comprehensive or limited — without compromising dignity and sovereignty.' The proposal came amid stalled negotiations: Russia continues demanding full recognition of all four annexed oblasts and refuses to commit to a ceasefire as a precondition for talks. Separately, Zelensky concluded 'historic' security agreements with Saudi Arabia, the UAE, and Qatar during a Gulf tour, expanding Ukraine's diplomatic network beyond Western allies and discussing Ukraine's expertise in countering Iranian-designed drone systems. Prediction markets stood at 99.9% odds against a ceasefire by March 31. Zelensky also stated Ukraine wants a clear timeline for the next round of Russia talks.

title: Ukrainian Drone Strike Kills 1 in Taganrog, Russia; Debris Sparks Volgograd Refinery Fire; 102 UAVs Downed
detail: A Ukrainian drone strike killed at least 1 person and wounded several others in Taganrog, Rostov Oblast, damaging dozens of residential buildings. Separately, falling debris from a Ukrainian drone downed by Russian air defenses ignited a fire at an oil refinery in Volgograd Oblast. Russian authorities reported wounding 3 people (including 2 children) in Krasnodar Krai and multiple injuries in Belgorod region from Ukrainian drone activities. Russian air defense systems intercepted approximately 102 Ukrainian UAVs across multiple Russian regions in the 24-hour period. The nightly Ukrainian drone sortie rates have doubled in early 2026 to 100–200 aircraft per night compared to 50–70 aircraft in 2025, reflecting Ukraine's expanded long-range strike capability.
